hero.name: Richard J. Valeo, CSM
hero.title: Senior Scrum Master · Data-Driven Agile Delivery & Flow Optimization
hero.value: 15+ years leading Agile delivery across complex, cross-functional software initiatives — using velocity, WIP, cycle time, throughput, and cumulative flow data to drive sprint planning, forecasting, and continuous improvement, not just facilitate ceremonies.

the_case.requirement: 8+ years Scrum Master and/or Project Management experience
the_case.evidence: 15+ years of SM/delivery leadership across Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org, A&E, DigitasLBI/SunTrust, and more

the_case.requirement: 5+ years supporting Agile software development teams; deep SDLC/Scrum/Kanban knowledge
the_case.evidence: Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid Agile facilitation across every engagement since 2014, including SAFe/PI-aligned programs at Eptura and DigitasLBI/SunTrust

the_case.requirement: Strong experience using Agile metrics and reporting to guide sprint planning, forecasting, performance improvement
the_case.evidence: Eptura: built and maintained Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, WIP, and cumulative flow across a ~10-team program, driving 35%+ efficiency gain and 20% carryover reduction
Eptura (~10 distributed teams, India/California), Understood.org (12+ countries), A&E (~10 concurrent workstreams)
Certified Scrum Master (CSM), Scrum Alliance — current

the_case.requirement: Healthcare, life sciences, or regulated industry experience (preferred)
the_case.evidence: SOX-compliant delivery at Crocs (public retailer) and DigitasLBI/SunTrust (financial services) — not healthcare-specific, but genuine regulated-industry audit/change-control discipline

the_stack.description: Agile delivery and reporting tools used daily or with direct delivery experience

when_things_break.scenario: A program is heading toward a missed sprint commitment, but no one sees it coming until it's too late
when_things_break.response: At Eptura, I built Jira dashboards tracking velocity, sprint health, WIP, and cumulative flow across a ~10-team program — using that data to forecast delivery risk before it became a missed commitment, rather than finding out at the sprint review.

when_things_break.scenario: A data pipeline or delivery workflow has a hidden bottleneck no one has diagnosed
when_things_break.response: At a stealth-mode AI startup, I ran root cause analysis on a throughput bottleneck across a data processing pipeline and re-sequenced the workflow, improving throughput ~30% and strengthening delivery predictability.

when_things_break.scenario: Cross-environment release dependencies threaten a go-live and leadership has no visibility into readiness
when_things_break.response: At Crocs, I built 'Release Radar,' a Jira-based flow-visibility framework giving leadership real-time readiness data across every environment before dependencies could derail the go-live.

how_i_run_a_room: Every ceremony generates data — velocity, WIP, cycle time, cumulative flow — and that data drives the next planning decision, not just the retro discussion
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every stakeholder touchpoint; risks named before they become issues
Executive dashboards translate sprint-level data into program-level forecasting language leadership can act on
Root cause analysis on delivery misses — not just 'what happened' but 'what pattern will happen again if we don't fix the underlying cause'
Backlog readiness and WIP discipline enforced consistently across Scrum and Kanban teams alike
Go/no-go criteria and change-control gates defined before the sprint, not after it fails

sr_pm_partner.before: Sprint commitments slip without warning, ceremonies happen but don't generate decisions, and leadership finds out about delivery risk after it's already a problem
sr_pm_partner.after: Dashboards that forecast risk before it becomes a miss, ceremonies that produce real decisions, and a delivery culture where root cause analysis prevents the same bottleneck from recurring

the_ramp.day_one: Review current sprint/program health data (velocity, WIP, cumulative flow if available), shadow a ceremony for each team supported, and read existing backlog and RAID documentation
the_ramp.thirty_days: Own ceremony facilitation and reporting cadence for all supported teams; stand up or improve existing flow-metrics dashboards; identify 2-3 process improvements from initial root cause analysis of current delivery patterns
